subject: seaborn plotting functions
old: positional arguments allowed for most parameters
new: keyword-only arguments required for most parameters; only `data` is positional
truth: Stop passing positional arguments to seaborn plotting functions (except `data`); all other arguments must be keyword-only.
quote: Seaborn's plotting functions now require explicit keywords for most arguments, following the deprecation of positional arguments in v0.11.0.

from_fact: positional arguments allowed for most parameters
keyword-only arguments required for most parameters; only `data` is positional

subject: stripplot and swarmplot defaults
old: previous default behavior for categorical scatterplots
new: harmonized defaults with more recently introduced functions
truth: Stop relying on the old default behavior of stripplot and swarmplot; several defaults have changed to harmonize with newer functions.
quote: The updates also harmonize behavior with functions that have been more recently introduced. This should be relatively non-disruptive, although a few defaults will change

from_fact: previous default behavior for categorical scatterplots
harmonized defaults with more recently introduced functions

subject: categorical plots default color palette
old: A color palette is automatically applied to levels of the categorical variable by default
new: No color palette is applied by default; you must explicitly assign `hue` to see multiple colors
truth: Stop relying on automatic coloring of categorical variables; you must now explicitly assign the same variable to both x/y and hue to get multiple colors.
quote: A color palette is no longer applied to levels of the categorical variable by default. It is now necessary to explicitly assign `hue` to see multiple colors (i.e., assign the same variable to `x`/`y` and `hue`).

from_fact: a color palette is automatically applied to levels of the categorical variable by default
no color palette is applied by default; you must explicitly assign `hue` to see multiple colors
x/y
associative: why is my plot one color

subject: numeric hue variables default mapping
old: Numeric hue variables received a categorical (discrete) color mapping by default
new: Numeric hue variables now receive a continuous color mapping by default
truth: Stop expecting discrete colors for numeric hue variables; they now get a continuous gradient by default — pass palette='deep' to restore old behavior.
quote: Numeric `hue` variables now receive a continuous mapping by default, using the same rules as :func:`scatterplot`. Pass `palette="deep"` to reproduce previous defaults.

from_fact: numeric hue variables received a categorical (discrete) color mapping by default
numeric hue variables now receive a continuous color mapping by default

subject: hue + color gradient palette
old: Assigning both `hue` and a `color` parameter produces a gradient palette (was valid)
new: Assigning both `hue` and `color` is now deprecated
truth: Stop passing both hue and color together to get a gradient palette; use palette='light:blue' instead as this combination is now deprecated.
quote: Currently, assigning `hue` and then passing a `color` will produce a gradient palette. This is now deprecated, as it is easy to request a gradient with, e.g. `palette="light:blue"`.

from_fact: assigning both `hue` and a `color` parameter produces a gradient palette (was valid)
assigning both `hue` and `color` is now deprecated
:blue

subject: ci parameter in lineplot/barplot/pointplot
old: `ci` parameter for error bars
new: `errorbar` parameter
truth: Stop using the `ci` parameter for lineplot, barplot, and pointplot; use the new `errorbar` parameter instead.
quote: As a consequence of this change, the `ci` parameter has been deprecated.

from_fact: `ci` parameter for error bars
`errorbar` parameter

subject: set_palette / set_theme
old: set_palette (or set_theme) used to set the patch.facecolor rc param
new: patch.facecolor rc param is no longer set by set_palette or set_theme
truth: Stop relying on set_palette or set_theme to set the patch.facecolor rc parameter; the matplotlib default 'C0' is now used instead.
quote: The `patch.facecolor` rc param is no longer set by :func:`set_palette` (or :func:`set_theme`). This should have no general effect, because the matplotlib default is now `"C0"`

from_fact: set_palette (or set_theme) used to set the patch.facecolor rc param
patch.facecolor rc param is no longer set by set_palette or set_theme
patch.facecolor

subject: scipy dependency
old: scipy was a required dependency
new: scipy is now optional; use pip install seaborn[stats] to get scipy and statsmodels
truth: Stop assuming scipy is always available; install seaborn[stats] if your code depends on scipy or statsmodels.
quote: Made `scipy` an optional dependency and added `pip install seaborn[stats]` as a method for ensuring the availability of compatible `scipy` and `statsmodels` libraries at install time.

from_fact: scipy was a required dependency
scipy is now optional; use pip install seaborn[stats] to get scipy and statsmodels

subject: example datasets storage location
old: example datasets were stored in ~/seaborn-data (user's home directory)
new: example datasets are now stored in an OS-specific cache location determined by appdirs
truth: Stop expecting example datasets at ~/seaborn-data; they are now stored in an OS-specific appdirs cache location.
quote: Example datasets are now stored in an OS-specific cache location (as determined by `appdirs`) rather than in the user's home directory. Users should feel free to remove `~/seaborn-data` if desired

from_fact: example datasets were stored in ~/seaborn-data (user's home directory)
example datasets are now stored in an os-specific cache location determined by appdirs

subject: factorplot
old: factorplot()
truth: Stop using factorplot() as it has been removed; use catplot() instead.
quote: Removed the previously-deprecated `factorplot`

from_fact: factorplot()
catplot()

subject: utility functions iqr, percentiles, pmf_hist, sort_df
old: iqr(), percentiles(), pmf_hist(), sort_df()
truth: Stop using iqr(), percentiles(), pmf_hist(), and sort_df() as they have been removed.
quote: |API| Removed the previously-deprecated `factorplot` along with several previously-deprecated utility functions (`iqr`, `percentiles`, `pmf_hist`, and `sort_df`).

from_fact: iqr(), percentiles(), pmf_hist(), sort_df()
iqr()
percentiles()
pmf_hist()
sort_df()

subject: pointplot additional keyword arguments
old: passing extra **kwargs to pointplot()
truth: Stop passing additional keyword arguments to pointplot() as that option has been removed.
quote: |API| Removed the (previously-unused) option to pass additional keyword arguments to :func:`pointplot`.

from_fact: passing extra **kwargs to pointplot()
pointplot()

subject: categorical plot default colors
old: categorical plots (boxplot, etc.) used multiple colors from the palette by default even without hue assigned
new: categorical plots now use a single main color unless hue is explicitly assigned
truth: Categorical plots no longer assign multiple colors by default; to get the old multi-color look, explicitly pass hue (e.g., hue='x').
quote: The categorical functions now act more like the rest of seaborn in that they will produce a plot with a single main color unless the `hue` variable is assigned. Previously, there would be an implicit redundant color mapping

from_fact: categorical plots (boxplot, etc.) used multiple colors from the palette by default even without hue assigned
categorical plots now use a single main color unless hue is explicitly assigned
e.g

subject: palette without hue
old: Passing a palette without explicitly assigning hue
truth: Stop passing a palette without an explicit hue assignment; add an explicitly redundant hue assignment instead.
quote: Passing a palette without explicitly assigning hue is no longer supported (add an explicitly redundant hue assignment instead).

from_fact: passing a palette without explicitly assigning hue

subject: color with hue for gradient
old: Passing a color while assigning hue to produce a gradient
truth: Stop passing a color while assigning hue to produce a gradient; use palette="dark:{color}" or palette="light:{color}" instead.
quote: Passing a color while assigning hue to produce a gradient is no longer supported (use palette="dark:{color}" or palette="light:{color}" instead).

from_fact: passing a color while assigning hue to produce a gradient

subject: default palette for numeric data
old: default palette was always qualitative (e.g. 'deep')
new: default palette now depends on variable type; sequential palette used with numeric data
truth: The default palette now depends on variable type, using a sequential palette for numeric data instead of a qualitative one; pass palette="deep" to retain old behavior.
quote: the default palette now depends on the variable type, and a sequential palette will be used with numeric data. To retain the previous behavior, pass the name of a qualitative palette (e.g., palette="deep" for seaborn's default).

from_fact: default palette was always qualitative (e.g. 'deep')
default palette now depends on variable type; sequential palette used with numeric data

subject: gray string for automatic gray
old: Using "gray" to select an automatic gray value that complements the main palette
new: Use "auto" instead
truth: Stop using "gray" to select an automatic gray value; use "auto" instead.
quote: Using "gray" to select an automatic gray value that complements the main palette is now deprecated in favor of "auto".

from_fact: using "gray" to select an automatic gray value that complements the main palette
use "auto" instead

subject: pointplot parameters
old: scale, join, errwidth parameters
new: pass keyword arguments directly to the underlying matplotlib artist
truth: Stop using `scale`, `join`, and `errwidth` in pointplot; pass keyword arguments directly to customize the Line2D artist instead.
quote: parameters that previously allowed only partial customization (`scale`, `join`, and `errwidth`) are now deprecated

from_fact: scale, join, errwidth parameters
pass keyword arguments directly to the underlying matplotlib artist

subject: violinplot bandwidth parameter
old: bw parameter
new: bw_method and bw_adjust parameters
truth: Stop using `bw` in violinplot; use `bw_method` and `bw_adjust` instead.
quote: the `bw` parameter is now deprecated in favor of `bw_method` and `bw_adjust`

from_fact: bw parameter
bw_method and bw_adjust parameters

subject: barplot error bar parameters
old: errcolor and errwidth parameters
new: err_kws dictionary parameter
truth: Stop using `errcolor` and `errwidth` in barplot; use the `err_kws` dictionary parameter instead.
quote: the `errcolor` and `errwidth` parameters are now deprecated in favor of a more general `err_kws` dictionary

from_fact: errcolor and errwidth parameters
err_kws dictionary parameter

subject: violinplot scale parameters
old: scale and scale_hue parameters
new: density_norm and common_norm parameters
truth: Stop using `scale` and `scale_hue` in violinplot; use `density_norm` and `common_norm` instead.
quote: the `scale` and `scale_hue` parameters have been renamed to `density_norm` and `common_norm`

from_fact: scale and scale_hue parameters
density_norm and common_norm parameters

subject: boxenplot scale parameter
old: scale parameter
new: width_method parameter
truth: Stop using `scale` in boxenplot; use `width_method` instead.
quote: the `scale` parameter has been renamed to `width_method`

from_fact: scale parameter
width_method parameter

subject: barplot/pointplot data parameter with vector
old: passing a vector to `data` plotted a single aggregated value
new: passing a vector to `data` draws a bar/point for each entry
truth: Passing a vector to the `data` parameter of barplot or pointplot now draws one bar/point per entry instead of aggregating; assign the vector to `y` to retain old behavior.
quote: |Defaults| |Enhancement| When passing a vector to the `data` parameter of :func:`barplot` or :func:`pointplot`, a bar or point will be drawn for each entry in the vector rather than plotting a single aggregated value.

from_fact: passing a vector to `data` plotted a single aggregated value
passing a vector to `data` draws a bar/point for each entry
bar/point

subject: boxenplot artist structure
old: boxen drawn as single patches
new: boxen drawn with separate patch artists in each tail
truth: Boxenplot now draws separate patch artists per tail, which breaks code that accesses underlying artists.
quote: the boxen are now drawn with separate patch artists in each tail. This may have consequences for code that works with the underlying artists

from_fact: boxen drawn as single patches
boxen drawn with separate patch artists in each tail

subject: boxenplot luminance ramp
old: inconsistent luminance ramp across plots
new: consistent luminance ramp for all box levels
truth: Boxenplot now uses a consistent luminance ramp, changing the appearance of existing plots.
quote: all plots now use a consistent luminance ramp for the different box levels. This leads to a change in the appearance of existing plots

from_fact: inconsistent luminance ramp across plots
consistent luminance ramp for all box levels

subject: boxenplot area scaling
old: area scaling was poorly defined
new: area scaling now approximates density of underlying observations
truth: Boxenplot with `width_method="area"` now approximates observation density, substantially changing plot appearance for asymmetric distributions.
quote: |Defaults| |Enhancement| The `"area"` scaling in :func:`boxenplot` now approximates the density of the underlying observations, including for asymmetric distributions.

from_fact: area scaling was poorly defined
area scaling now approximates density of underlying observations

subject: histplot binwidth
old: binwidth parameter produced exact bin widths
new: binwidth now produces only approximately equal bin widths when the value does not evenly divide the bin range
truth: In histplot, binwidth now only approximately matches the requested width when the value does not evenly divide the bin range, instead of producing exact bin widths.
quote: In :func:`histplot`, treatment of the `binwidth` parameter has changed such that the actual bin width will be only approximately equal to the requested width when that value does not evenly divide the bin range.

from_fact: binwidth parameter produced exact bin widths
binwidth now produces only approximately equal bin widths when the value does not evenly divide the bin range

subject: data values outside scale transform domain
old: data values outside scale transform domain (e.g. non-positive with log scale) were not dropped before statistical operations
new: data values outside scale transform domain are now dropped prior to any statistical operations
truth: Data values outside the scale transform domain (e.g. non-positive values with a log scale) are now dropped before any statistical operations, which may change results for existing code.
quote: Data values outside the scale transform domain (e.g. non-positive values with a log scale) are now dropped prior to any statistical operations

from_fact: data values outside scale transform domain (e.g. non-positive with log scale) were not dropped before statistical operations
data values outside scale transform domain are now dropped prior to any statistical operations
e.g

subject: statistics space based on axes scales
old: statistics were performed in data space, with spotty support for log scales
new: statistics are now performed in the appropriate space based on axes scales
truth: Statistics are now performed in the appropriate space based on axes scales (e.g. log space for log scales), which changes the behavior of statistical computations for existing code using non-linear scales.
quote: There is now generalized support for performing statistics in the appropriate space based on axes scales; previously support for this was spotty and at best worked only for log scales

from_fact: statistics were performed in data space, with spotty support for log scales
statistics are now performed in the appropriate space based on axes scales
e.g
